text: Abstract Background Plant-derived exosomes-like nanovesicles (PDENs) have been shown great potentials for the development of new disease therapies and drug delivery system, yet the little is known about their biogenesis, compositions and the marker proteins of PDENs, which severely limits the large-scale production of PDENs. Moreover, the preparation method of PDENs is not yet efficient. In the present study, a novel PDENs-based chemotherapeutic and immunomodulatory material, Catharanthus roseus (L.) Don leaves-derived exosome-like nanovesicles (CLDENs) was prepared from apoplastic fluid. Results Their structure, physical and chemical properties, biological behavior were fully characterized. CLDENs have a membrane structure with average diameter of 75.51 ± 10.19 nm and negative charge of −21.8 mV. CLDENs exhibit great stability and can resist the multiple enzyme digestion, strong acidity or alkalinity and gastrointestinal fluid digestion, implying that CLDENs may enter into the gastrointestinal tract and then be absorbed by immune cells, further induced to take effect on the immune organs. In vitro and in vivo biodistribution experiments showed that CLDENs can be internalized by the immune cells in the lymph nodes and spleen after the intraperitoneal injection. Lipidomic analysis revealed the unique lipid composition of CLDENs which consisted of 36.5% ether-phospholipids and 30.4% other lipid components. Using differential proteomics, we confirmed that CLDENs were derived from multivesicular bodies, and six marker proteins of CLDENs were identified for the first time. CLDENs in concentrations ranging from 60 ~ 240 μg/ml promoted the polarization and phagocytic functions of macrophages and the proliferation of lymphocytes. In addition, the administration of CLDENs at the doses of 20 mg/kg and 60 mg/kg obviously restored the decreased white blood cells (WBCs) and promoted the progression of the cell cycle in the bone marrow cells of immunosuppressed mice induced by cyclophosphamide. In particular, CLDENs remarkably upregulated the secretion of TNF-α, activated NF-κB signal pathway and increased the expression of PU.1 transcription factor in both in vitro and in vivo experiments. To meet the large-scale need of CLDENs requirement, a plant cell culture system of C. roseus was established and plant cell suspension culture produced CLDENs-like nanovesicles (CLDENs-LNVs) similar to those derived from C. roseus leaves, which showed similar physical and biological properties to those of CLDENs. Moreover, plant cell suspension culture produced about three times more CLDENs-LNVs than those derived from C. roseus leaves. Conclusions Our results demonstrated that CLDENs could serve as a promising nano-biomaterial for the preclinical application of post-chemotherapy immunoadjuvant therapy.
